business.headline: Goods transport costs rise from today, daily essentials may soon get costlierTransporters have introduced a fuel-linked surcharge as diesel prices climb. The move could push up logistics costs for groceries, medicines and online deliveries.

RBI announces $5 billion swap auction amid rupee weaknessIn a circular, the RBI said it had decided to conduct a USD 5 billion USD/INR buy-sell swap auction after reviewing current and changing liquidity conditions.

Silver slips by more than Rs 4,000: 3 key reasons behind the sharp fallIf you have been tracking silver prices, Thursday may have come as a surprise. Silver dropped by over Rs 4,000 on the MCX, even as global uncertainty remained high. Here are the three key factors behind the sharp fall.

India's power demand hits fresh record for 3rd straight day amid heatwaveThe country's peak power demand touched 265.44 gigawatts (GW) at 3:45 pm on Wednesday, going past Tuesday's previous high of 260.45 GW, according to data shared by the Ministry of Power.

Could the rupee ever hit 150 against the US dollar?Most economists will still rule out the viral prediction about the rupee hitting 150 against the US dollar. But in a world shaped by wars, oil shocks and volatile capital flows, is the debate really that absurd anymore?

Nvidia cashes in on AI gold rush with record earnings and $80 billion buybackNvidia Q1 results: The chip giant reported another blockbuster quarter, posting record revenue and profit numbers as global technology companies continue spending heavily on AI infrastructure, data centres and advanced computing systems.

'Melody' buzz keeps fuelling the wrong Parle on D-Street as stock jumps 5% againParle Industries shares hit an upper circuit again after videos showed Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifting Melody toffees to Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. The move drew attention because the stock has no link to Parle Products, pointing to mistaken-identity buying.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude oil eases on hopes of Iran peace dealThe BSE Sensex jumped 345.02 points, or 0.46%, to 75,663.41 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 131.30 points, or 0.55%, to 23,790.30 as of 9:44 am.

Why have cab and auto drivers called a 3-day strike in Delhi-NCRMore than 68 transport unions have begun a three-day strike across Delhi-NCR, affecting cabs and autos. Drivers say rising fuel costs, stagnant fares and app commissions have pushed them under severe financial strain.

Silent listings? Why companies are going for confidential IPO filingsMore Indian companies are turning to Sebi's confidential IPO filing route as West Asia tensions unsettle markets. The option gives them more time and less public exposure before deciding when to launch.

James Murdoch to buy New York Magazine and Vox podcasts in $300 million dealJames Murdoch's Lupa Systems is acquiring New York Magazine, Vox's podcast network and Vox.com for more than $300 million. The deal adds a major magazine brand and sought-after podcast talent to his growing media portfolio.

Gita Gopinath says jobs, inflation bigger worry for India than rupee at 100As the West Asia conflict drags on, Gita Gopinath warns India may face sharper fuel hikes, rising inflation and a weaker rupee, while arguing the country still has enough economic strength to avoid panic.

Gita Gopinath discusses impact of West Asia conflict, rising oil prices and moreIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Gita Gopinath, Professor of Economics at Harvard University, discusses the impact of the West Asia conflict and rising oil prices on the Indian economy.

Sebi plans PF, NPS-like payroll-linked MF SIPs. Details hereInvesting in mutual funds through salary deductions, much like PF and NPS, may soon become possible. SEBI has proposed a new framework that could also allow donations through mutual fund units.

Pleasantly surprised: Parle VP reacts after PM Modi gifts Melody to MeloniPrime Minister Narendra Modi's gift of Melody toffee to Giorgia Meloni during his Italy trip gave fresh attention to the 'Melodi' buzz, leaving Parle Products pleasantly surprised by the global attention.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite rupee hitting fresh record lowThe BSE Sensex rose 117.54 points, or 0.16%, to close at 75,318.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 41 points, or 0.17%, to settle at 23,659.

Gold loans surge 50% in FY26: Why more Indians are borrowing against goldNeed money urgently but do not want the hassle of lengthy paperwork? Many Indians are increasingly turning to gold loans as a quick and easier way to raise funds, especially at a time when gold prices remain strong.

Parle Industries jumps 5% after Modi-Meloni Melody buzz, but there's a catchSocial media buzz around PM Modi gifting Melody to Italy PM Giorgia Meloni appears to have sparked confusion among retail investors, sending Parle Industries shares sharply higher despite no business connection to the toffee brand. Parle Products is the firm producing the Melody toffee.

Banks may soon have to share more details on risks and financial health, says RBIOn Tuesday, the RBI released a draft circular, wherein banks will be required to publish more detailed information on important financial measures such as capital adequacy, leverage, liquidity and risks.

Need PF money fast? EPFO may soon let you withdraw funds through UPILabour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya said on Tuesday that the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has completed testing for a facility that would allow members to withdraw their Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) using the UPI payment gateway.

entertainment.headline: Prime Video, Sony LIV, ZEE5 this week: System, Satrangi Badle Ka Khel and more New OTT releases on May 22 bring a mix of courtroom drama, romance, revenge, and mystery across Prime Video, Sony LIV, and ZEE5. Titles like System, Madhuvidhu, Satrangi, and Memu Copulam headline the week's fresh streaming lineup.

Drishyam 3 review: Mohanlal-Jeethu Joseph's sequel is less clever, more dramaticRelease Date:Thu May 21, 2026 Drishyam 3 movie review: Director Jeethu Joseph's Drishyam 3, starring Mohanlal, Meena and Siddique, is a sequel that prioritises drama over intelligence. While the film has some meaty ideas that could make for a brilliant thriller, it settles for less because of its writing.

Netflix this week: Dhurandhar Raw and Undekha, Ladies First and more Netflix is set to release a diverse slate of titles this week, including the uncut version of Dhurandhar, the sci-fi drama The Boroughs, the satirical film Ladies First and Bad Thoughts Season 2.

Saswata Chatterjee to play Sourav Ganguly's father Chandi Ganguly in biopic Saswata Chatterjee has begun filming in Mumbai as Sourav Ganguly's father in the cricketer's biopic. The shoot has now moved into production with key family roles cast and the Kolkata schedule due this month.

Anurag Kashyap, Dia Mirza follow viral Cockroach Janta Party on Instagram From Anurag Kashyap to Dia Mirza, several celebrities have joined the online buzz around the viral Cockroach Janta Party. Here's why the satirical movement is taking over social media.

Adnan Sami reacts to trolls after sharing pics with Mohan Bhagwat: I am a free soul Adnan Sami reacted to the trolling he faced after sharing pictures with RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat. He said he does not care about public judgment or online criticism.

Bandar trailer: Bobby Deol battles fame, loneliness and suspicion The trailer of Bandar starring Bobby Deol is finally out, offering a tense glimpse into Anurag Kashyap's dark thriller packed with mystery, crime and fading stardom.

Dhurandhar gets a 'raw and undekha' cut, fans can stream it from this date Netflix has announced that Dhurandhar Raw and Undekha will stream from May 22. The release adds a fresh turn to the franchise's staggered digital rollout across platforms and markets.

Jr NTR plays anti-hero in Dragon, but director says there's a patriotic twist Director Prashanth Neel says Jr NTR's Dragon could become one of the most patriotic movies ever seen. He added that the teaser only reveals the film's setting, not its real emotional core.

Drishyam 3 Day 1 box office: Packed shows, huge buzz, Rs 30 crore hopes for Mohanlal Can Mohanlal and Georgekutty pull off one more masterstroke? As Drishyam 3 opens to massive frenzy and soaring expectations, trade experts decode the pressure, the box office stakes, and why this could become Malayalam cinema's next big milestone.

Jasmine Sandlas halts Dehradun show after barricade mishap, apologises to fans Jasmine Sandlas halted her Dehradun concert after a barricade near the front rows gave way, causing attendees to fall. The singer apologised, asked her team to step in and raised concerns about aggressive security.

Pritam hits back in Cocktail 2 song row, calls critics unpaid PR Pritam has pushed back against online plagiarism allegations around Mashooqa from Cocktail 2. The row has fuelled a wider debate over copying, inspiration and sampling on social media.

Drishyam 3 X reviews: Fans declare Mohanlal's film a blockbuster already Drishyam 3 has opened to enthusiastic reactions on X, with fans already calling Mohanlal's latest thriller a "blockbuster" after its first-day screenings. Viewers particularly praised the film's gripping interval block, emotional moments and Mohanlal's powerful return as Georgekutty.

Drishyam 3 first day first show turns into birthday carnival for Mohanlal fans. Watch Mohanlal's 66th birthday turned grand with Drishyam 3 release, prompting packed theatres and fan celebrations across venues. Viral videos from various theatres across Kerala showed how fans turned the release into a carnival.

Shah Rukh Khan's King faces AI leak crisis as makers tighten security The makers of Shah Rukh Khan's King have tightened security after an AI-generated fan edit went viral using leaked material. The move follows fresh leak concerns as the team pushes to protect the film's final schedule.

Karuppu box office Day 6: Suriya film beats Retro, nears Rs 200 crore mark globally Suriya's Karuppu crossed Rs 175.96 crore worldwide on its sixth day in theatres. The Tamil version led collections as the film sustained strong audience support despite mixed reviews.

Pati Patni Aur...box office Day 6: Film sees weekday dip, nears Rs 40 crore globally Pati Patni Aur Woh Do posed Rs 2.75 crore nett in India on Day 6 as its run stayed steady. The latest numbers took its worldwide gross to Rs 37.42 crore, keeping it on track for Rs 40 crore before the weekend.

Desi Bling review: Rage bait for bank balance, comfort food for soulRelease Date:Wed May 20, 2026 Desi Bling review: Netflix's Desi Bling follows Dubai's wealthy Indian social circle through opulence, gossip and on-camera confrontations. Beneath the designer spectacle, the series reveals lonely relationships, regressive attitudes and carefully staged emotion.

Suriya's Karuppu: Whistle Podu hit for Tamil cinema amid Vijay's Jana Nayagan wait Suriya's Karuppu grossed Rs 161 crore globally in five days. The film's run gives Kollywood the boost Vijay's Jana Nayagan was expected to, had it not been delayed.

general news.headline: Cockroach Party's X account blocked hours after it beats BJP in Instagram followersThis comes just hours after the CJP crossed 10 million followers on Instagram, overtaking the BJP's official account, which currently has around 8.7 million followers on the platform.

Abhishek Banerjee gets court protection in case over remarks on Amit ShahAn FIR was registered against Abhishek Banerjee at Bidhannagar Cyber Crime Police Station over alleged provocative speeches during the 2026 West Bengal election campaign. The complaint, also naming Union Home Minister Amit Shah, alleges inflammatory remarks, threats and attempts to disturb public tranquillity.

Astrologer to tank tender: Vijay's first-week jitters and rollbacksVijay's opening week as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister has been marked by controversies and quick rollbacks of government orders. It remains to be seen whether these are merely first-week jitters or signs of a newbie TVK government struggling to balance optics, governance and coalition pangs.

What is Abhishek Banerjee-linked 'Bhaipo tax' Bengal is cracking down on?The Suvendu Adhikari-led BJP government has ordered action against illegal highway collection points, branded the 'Bhaipo tax' in Bengal. The move follows Amit Shah's campaign promise in the run-up to the polls.

Mark Zuckerberg sends note to laid-off H-1B employees: We know this is difficultMeta has laid off nearly 8,000 employees as part of its AI-focused restructuring, leaving many H-1B employees worried about their future in the US. In the layoff memo, CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged the struggles of visa-sponsored employees, saying, "We know this is especially difficult."
Rains on approach: When will monsoon hit India? Latest forecast hereAs North India reels under brutal heatwave conditions, all eyes are now on a weather system gathering strength far to the South.

Can't curb political criticism: Court on Raghav Chadha's personality rights pleaThe Delhi High Court observed that criticism, satire and political cartoons targeting politicians cannot be curbed merely because they may be unpalatable to political leaders.

Beer, champagne and cigarettes? 1983 World Cup winner debunks viral party billFormer India cricketer Kirti Azad dismissed the viral restaurant bill linked to India's 1983 World Cup celebrations as fake, asserting that the team never visited the hotel mentioned in the receipt and that Kapil Dev's signature on the document had been forged.

Cockroach party beats BJP on the 'gram with 10 million followers in 5 daysA satirical political campaign, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), born online after the Chief Justice of India's remarks on unemployed youth, has crossed 10 million Instagram followers in five days. The Abhijeet Dipke-founded CJP has managed to surpass the ruling BJP's follower count.

How China built world's largest Chongqing rail station in 38 months with robotsIn just 38 months, China built the world's largest railway station in Chongqing, a sprawling megacity strategically developed under Mao Zedong's Third Front campaign. Beijing could make the unthinkable possible due to a robot revolution.

PM calls crucial council meeting today, all ministers asked to stay in DelhiAll Union ministers have been asked to remain in Delhi ahead of a crucial meeting chaired by PM Modi, where governance, the West Asia crisis and possible political changes are expected to dominate discussions.

Can the CJP use cockroach as its poll symbol? Here's a look at ECI's poll-symbols list and the rules.

Mamata Banerjee lost even in her own ward, not just across BhabanipurFresh Election Commission data shows Suvendu Adhikari fulfilled Amit Shah's "ghar mein harana" call by crushing Mamata Banerjee in her own ward, home to her Kalighat residence, securing 63 per cent votes to her 30 per cent, while she failed to cross even 50 votes in several booths across the constituency.

Twisha Sharma's mother-in-law called people in judiciary after death: FamilyTwisha Sharma's family alleged that her retired judge mother-in-law made calls to people linked to the judiciary and CCTV installers after the 33-year-old's death.

A Manipuri silk stole and more: Inside PM Modi's gifts to Italy's MeloniAt diplomatic meetings in Italy, Prime Minister Narendra Modi presented gifts rooted in India's regional crafts and traditions. The selection highlighted India's cultural diversity and soft power through textiles, music, craftsmanship and food.
Rain coming? Satellite shows new western disturbance approaching IndiaFresh IMD satellite images show a strong western disturbance moving towards north India amid a severe heatwave.

After schools, Vande Mataram now compulsory in madrasas in West BengalWest Bengal has made Vande Mataram compulsory in all recognised madrasas during morning assemblies. The move comes just a week after a similar order was issued for schools under the School Education Department.

sports.headline: Nabi vs Brar: Why was one of India's best pacers not given Test debut? Jammu and Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi guided his team to a historic Ranji Trophy victory this season. The incredible red-ball performance got him a white-ball IPL contract, but was not deemed good enough for the Indian Test team.

Novak Djokovic announces new head coach ahead of French Open: Who is Viktor Troicki? Novak Djokovic has appointed Viktor Troicki as his new head coach before the French Open. The move reunites two long-time Serbian allies as he chases a record 25th Grand Slam title.

Hardik Pandya sanctioned for misconduct during Mumbai Indians' loss to KKR Hardik Pandya was fined 10 per cent of his match fee and received one demerit point after the Mumbai Indians captain forcefully dislodged the bails during the IPL 2026 match against Kolkata Knight Riders at the Eden Gardens on Wednesday.

Playing through pain: Varun refuses to take the easy option, says Shane Watson KKR spinner Varun Chakravarthy should ideally be resting but simply refuses to give up on the team's campaign, said assistant coach Shane Watson. KKR have made an incredible comeback in the second half of the season after remaining winless for the first 6 games this season.

Beer, champagne and cigarettes? 1983 World Cup winner debunks viral party bill Former India cricketer Kirti Azad dismissed the viral restaurant bill linked to India's 1983 World Cup celebrations as fake, asserting that the team never visited the hotel mentioned in the receipt and that Kapil Dev's signature on the document had been forged.

East Bengal, Mohun Bagan headline four-way ISL title battle on dramatic final day East Bengal and Mohun Bagan headline a dramatic final day in the ISL title race, but Punjab FC and Mumbai City FC are still very much alive too. With four clubs separated by the finest margins, Thursday night could swing wildly with every goal across Kolkata and New Delhi.

Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma not automatic choices for 2027 ODI World Cup: Sources Ajit Agarkar and his selection panel deliberated resting Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma for the upcoming three-match ODI series. With the ODI World Cup more than a year away, selection will be based on consistent performances, sources said.

GT vs CSK preview: Can Chennai keep their stuttering hopes alive in Ahmedabad? IPL 2026: Destiny isn't in the hands of CSK as they look to keep their fading playoff hopes alive in Ahmedabad against GT. Gujarat will be aiming to get back to winning ways and tighten up their performance in front of their home crowd.

Cricket Canada controversy: Gunshots fired at board president's house The controversy surrounding Cricket Canada took a shocking turn after gunshots were fired at the house of newly elected board president Arvinder Khosa. The incident comes amid ongoing investigations into alleged match-fixing, corruption and suspected links between figures associated with Canadian cricket and the Lawrence Bishnoi gang.

All roads lead to 'Villa' for Europa League king Unai Emery Aston Villa won their first Europa League title on Wednesday, May 20, under the coaching of Unai Emery. This was the Spanish coach's fifth Europa trophy, funnily with clubs having 'Villa' in their name.

No trip to Ahmedabad? MS Dhoni heads to Ranchi on CSK's final matchday IPL 2026: MS Dhoni left for Ranchi on CSK's final league matchday after suffering a thumb injury. Chennai Super Kings will take on Gujarat Titans in their final league game with the play-offs hopes hanging by a thread.

The forgotten IPL OG: How cricket repaid Manish Pandey in KolkataIPL 2026: He has played all 19 IPL seasons, batted just 28 times in six years, and never stopped showing up. On a tricky Eden Gardens pitch, with KKR's playoff dream on the line, Manish Pandey finally got his night.

IPL Play of the Day: Sunil Narine weaves his magic again to keep Kolkata afloatIPL 2026, KKR vs MI: Sunil Narine produced another outstanding performance for Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday as the three-time champions kept their playoff hopes alive with a four-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians at the Eden Gardens.

Hardik Pandya rues fielding collapse behind MI's disastrous IPL 2026 seasonIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya admitted Mumbai Indians' poor fielding standards became one of the biggest reasons behind their disastrous IPL 2026 campaign. After another sloppy performance against KKR, MI's season now heads towards an underwhelming finish with serious questions looming over the franchise.

Manish Pandey repays KKR faith with match-winning MI knock: That's why I'm hereIPL 2026: Manish Pandey finally got his first real batting opportunity of IPL 2026 and instantly turned back the clock for KKR. On a tense Eden Gardens night, the veteran repaid Kolkata's faith with a vintage chase-masterclass that kept their playoff hopes alive.

IPL playoffs scenario: How Kolkata Knight Riders can still seal 4th spotIPL 2026, KKR vs MI: Kolkata Knight Riders have given themselves a great chance of qualifying for the playoffs after beating the Mumbai Indians by four wickets on Wednesday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Who came in for Angkrish Raghuvanshi vs MI? KKR's concussion sub sparks debateIPL 2026: KKR's handling of Angkrish Raghuvanshi's substitution during the win over Mumbai Indians triggered confusion. The lack of clarity over a reported concussion replacement turned the match into a rules debate.

KKR vs MI: A Manish Pandey classic at the Eden keeps Kolkata in playoff raceManish Pandey steadied Kolkata Knight Riders after an early wobble and carried the chase against Mumbai Indians to help them remain in the hunt for the IPL 2026 playoffs. MI's batting woes continued with the bat, and they struggled with their catching as Mumbai lost their ninth match of the season.

Couple goals: Gael Monfils, Elina Svitolina train together at French OpenTennis power couple Gael Monfils and Elina Svitolina thrilled fans after training together at Roland Garros, with their practice session quickly going viral on social media. Fresh off her Italian Open triumph, Svitolina heads into the French Open in top form, while Monfils is set to enjoy massive home support in Paris.

MS Dhoni taught me confidence without overconfidence: CSK star Dewald BrevisIPL 2026: Dewald Brevis said MS Dhoni's advice at CSK helped him understand the line between confidence and overconfidence. The South African batter said Dhoni's humility and presence remained influential despite his injury-hit season.
Chahar recreates note celebration after dismissing Finn Allen, but with a twistDeepak Chahar dismissed Finn Allen and then mimed his own version of the note celebration at Eden Gardens.

Rishabh Pant did nothing wrong: Kaif on wicketkeeper losing Test vice-captaincyMohammed Kaif has criticised the selectors for removing Rishabh Pant as India's Test vice-captain before the Afghanistan series. He said using IPL form to judge a proven red-ball match-winner exposed inconsistency in selection thinking.

French Open tensions rise as players plan media protest over prize money structureTop tennis stars are planning a media protest at the French Open over prize-money distribution, with players reportedly limiting press interactions during Roland Garros. The protest comes as players push for a bigger share of Grand Slam revenues.

Mohun Bagan among seven clubs denied AIFF license: What happens now?AIFF's club licensing body rejected the Premier 1 applications of seven clubs, including Mohun Bagan Super Giant, for 2026-27. The decision leaves them temporarily ineligible for national and AFC competitions unless they secure clearance in time.

No FIFA World Cup 2026 for Indian fans? Doordarshan says no to broadcast battlePrasar Bharati told the Delhi High Court it is not responsible for acquiring FIFA World Cup rights in India. The stand has deepened uncertainty over how Indian fans will watch the tournament before the June 12 start.

Mohammad Amir gets British citizenship. Is ex-Pakistan pacer eligible for IPL?Former Pakistan fast bowler Mohammad Amir is now eligible to play in the IPL after acquiring British citizenship, potentially allowing him to enter the league as an overseas player from 2027.

We are not done: Pat Cummins makes bold claim on Australia pace trio's futureSpeaking to India Today, Pat Cummins said the legendary trio of himself, Mitchell Starc, and Josh Hazlewood have no plans to retire anytime soon, targeting a monumental upcoming calendar that includes the ODI World Cup, the Ashes in England and a Test series in India.

RCB should never take Virat Kohli for granted: Mo Bobat on star's impactVirat Kohli's runs continue to headline RCB's IPL 2026 campaign, but the franchise believes his biggest contribution still goes far beyond statistics. RCB Director of Cricket Mo Bobat called Kohli the emotional and cultural engine driving the defending champions forward.

Ranji Trophy to begin on October 11 as BCCI announces 1,788-match domestic scheduleThe BCCI has announced a 1,788-match domestic calendar for 2026-27 across senior and junior cricket. It restores key fixtures, turns select U-23 events into T20 format.

Would you treat Bumrah the same way as Shami? Ex-India batter slams Ajit AgarkarWasim Jaffer has criticised Ajit Agarkar and the BCCI after Mohammed Shami was left out again for the upcoming series against Afghanistan. He called the explanation disrespectful and questioned whether Jasprit Bumrah would be treated the same way.

Ex-US Open champion wins hearts with touching gesture for heartbroken rivalFormer US Open champion Bianca Andreescu earned widespread praise after consoling tearful French teenager Daphnee Mpetshi Perricard following their French Open qualifying clash in Paris.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
